critic_raw: Four issues: preprocessing leakage, temporal split violation, multi-confounded model comparison, and unspecified statistical test for AUC comparison.
all_issues_raised: Preprocessing transformers fit on entire dataset before splitting — data leakage
Stratified random split on 5-year longitudinal EHR data ignores temporal ordering
Transformer fine-tuned end-to-end while baseline uses fixed structured features — multiple confounded advantages (text, end-to-end training, deep architecture) prevent attribution
Statistical test for AUC-ROC comparison not specified — DeLong's test vs. bootstrap difference test should be pre-specified

round_transcripts.critic: Four issues: preprocessing leakage, temporal split violation, multi-confounded model comparison, and unspecified statistical test for AUC comparison.
round_transcripts.defender:
**On Issue 1 (Preprocessing leakage):** Confirmed by explicit design text — preprocessing fit on combined dataset before splitting.

**On Issue 2 (Temporal split):** Confirmed — random split on 5-year EHR data violates causal ordering.

**On Issue 3 (Model comparison confounds):** The transformer model uses end-to-end fine-tuned BioBERT plus structured features; the baseline uses only structured features. The design conflates architecture (transformer vs. linear), modality (text vs. no text), and training regime (end-to-end vs. fixed). This is a valid multi-confound issue that limits causal attribution of performance differences.

**On Issue 4 (Statistical test):** The protocol mentions bootstrap confidence intervals for primary metrics but does not specify a paired statistical test for comparing two AUC values on the same test set. This is a minor gap — bootstrap difference tests are valid even if DeLong's test is more efficient — but the test should be pre-specified to prevent post-hoc selection.

round_transcripts.critic: All four issues confirmed. Issues 1, 2, and 3 are material design flaws. Issue 4 is a minor gap in protocol specification. Issues 1 and 2 are the most fundamental (data leakage and temporal leakage).
round_transcripts.defender: All four issues stand. Issues 1 and 2 are the most material. Issue 3 limits the interpretability of any observed result. Issue 4 is a minor gap.

all_issues_adjudicated: Preprocessing transformers fit on entire dataset before splitting — data leakage
Stratified random split on 5-year longitudinal EHR data ignores temporal ordering
Transformer fine-tuned end-to-end while baseline uses fixed structured features — multiple confounded advantages prevent attribution
